title: Open-Source Acknowledgment
description: Onboarding acknowledgment form ensuring developers comply with GPL, MIT, and Apache licenses, preventing copyleft contamination.
whatsCovered: Approved open-source licenses list (MIT, Apache, BSD)
Prohibition on using copyleft licenses (GPL/AGPL) without review
Procedure for documenting software library credits
Auditing of codebases using dependency trackers
whenToUse: Use this developer compliance form to forbid employees from compiling copyleft code (GPL) into proprietary products.

clauses.heading: OPEN-SOURCE ACKNOWLEDGMENT
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Open-Source Acknowledgment. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. Copyleft Code Restrictions
clauses.body: Developers are prohibited from using copyleft GPL code without review by: {compliance_audit_team}. All packages must follow approved lists.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
